rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Questions, Moving Party Ralph Oroquita
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 Further, the Court finds that Petitioner has not complied with statutory meet and confer requirement prior to filing this motion. A motion brought under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.480 must be "accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.480(b).) Per Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040, "A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion." Counsel's declaration states that he met and conferred with Respondent's counsel during the deposition, and sent a letter, attached as Exhibit B to the declaration. (Reed Decl. at para. 8). The Court has reviewed the letter submitted as Exhibit B. The letter consists of one paragraph and one final sentence. It does not (1) present each issue presented in the motion, or (2) attempt an informal resolution of the issues. Instead, the letter concludes: "If you do not provide that information [answers to two questions posed in the preceding paragraph] to me within 10 days, I will file a motion to compel the answer." (Reed Decl., Exh. B.) There is no showing in counsel's declaration or this exhibit that Petitioner engaged in "a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented in the motion." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040.) The request for monetary sanctions is also DENIED. The Notice of Motion must contain a request for sanctions and must: name all persons, parties and attorneys against whom sanctions are being sought; specify the type of sanction sought; cite the authority for such sanctions; and be accompanied by a declaration "setting forth facts supporting the amount" of the monetary sanction sought. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.040.) The Notice of Motion before the Court does not contain a request for sanctions or any of the required statutory elements. Counsel's declaration "[sets] forth facts supporting the amount" of sanctions sought. This alone is not sufficient notice of the request for sanctions. For example, it does not even specify against whom sanctions are being sought. Where sanctions are sought against the opposing party's counsel, the notice of motion must expressly so state. It is not enough simply to attach declarations or a transcript showing that the deponent refused to answer questions on counsel's advice. (See, e.g., Blumenthal v. Sup.Ct. (Corey) (1980) 103 CA3d 317, 320.) Respondent is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. Part 2 of 2

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RALPH R. OROQUITA REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Deposition Questions
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Petitioner Ralph Richard Oroquita's Motion to Compel Response to Deposition Questions. No opposition to the motion was filed. The motion is DENIED as procedurally defective. Under Cal. Civil Rules, Rule 3.1345, except in circumstances not presented here, "any motion involving the content of a discovery request or the responses to such a request must be accompanied by a separate statement. The motions that require a separate statement include a motion [t]o compel answers at a deposition." Rule 3.1345(c) goes on to explain, "A separate statement is a separate document filed and served with the discovery motion that provides all the information necessary to understand each discovery request and all the responses to it that are at issue. The separate statement must be full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response. Material must not be incorporated into the separate statement by reference. The separate statement must include for each discovery request [ ] the following: (1) The text of the request, interrogatory, question, or inspection demand; (2) The text of each response, answer, or objection, and any further responses or answers; (3) A statement of the factual and legal reasons for compelling further responses, answers, or production as to each matter in dispute; (4) If necessary, the text of all definitions, instructions, and other matters required to understand each discovery request and the responses to it; (5) If the response to a particular discovery request is dependent on the response given to another discovery request, or if the reasons a further response to a particular discovery request is deemed necessary are based on the response to some other discovery request, the other request and the response to it must be set forth; and (6) If the pleadings, other documents in the file, or other items of discovery are relevant to the motion, the party relying on them must summarize each relevant document. (d) Identification of interrogatories, demands, or requests Petitioner has not submitted a separate statement with this motion to compel responses to deposition questions. Instead, counsel has submitted a declaration summarizing the questions and responses now at issue in this motion and included excerpts of the deposition transcript as an exhibit. This does not satisfy the enumerated elements of a separate statement under Rule 3.1345 (see, e.g., sub.(c)(1) through (3)). Part 1 of 2
